title: ICE IN THE RIVER
subtitle: The Riverboat · 11:14 PM · Late February
epigraph_upright: The river has ice in it for the first time in a decade. A drifter is at the gangway who wasn't on the list. The river widow is already in the lounge. The radio is on in the wheelhouse without Nicola turning it on. The boat is doing what a boat does when the water around it is starting to behave like land.
epigraph_reversed: The drifter doesn't come aboard. The river widow leaves the way she came. The radio loses its signal. The ice presses harder against the hull. Tomorrow the boat will be where it was, but at a different angle, and the river will be in a different shape.
scene_description: Late February. 11:14 PM. Bloom is at 0 (deep winter; nothing grew). Harvest is at 2 (the pantry holds). Doubt at 2; stagnation at 2; one neighbor lost interest in coming. The drifter is at the gangway already, asking. The river widow is in the lounge. The radio is on.
direction_hint: Land four connections, two of them hard moods (drift + widow). Bloom can stay at 0 — it doesn't matter tonight. Get to a threshold before the ice presses.
opening_log_lines: the river has ice in it. you can see it from the bow window.
the drifter is at the gangway. they have not stepped aboard.
the radio is on. nicola did not turn it on.

stagnation_thresholds.label: winter boat. the ice does its thing.
the boat is leaning a fraction.
the hull plate at the engine room is talking.
the river found a way in.

doubt_thresholds.label: nicola in the wheelhouse, radio on.
she questions whether to let the drifter aboard.

notes: Hard-mode Empress. Winter, ice, drifter at the gangway. The hull-plate loss (river_takes_the_bank_after_turn 7) is the doom-clock. 4 connections + 2 hard moods (drift is left_alone; widow is lonely; Dante preoccupied) + leap.
ambient_pool: the ice presses. the boat answers in small sounds.
the radio in the wheelhouse is finding stations it didn't know it had.
the river door is bolted but you can feel cold air through the gap.
